vlm.uncertainty_reason: The after imagery is too blurred, low-resolution, and tonally different to confirm or rule out damage at this feature. Any apparent similarity/difference could be an artifact of the resolution and color shift rather than true ground change. A higher-resolution or better-resolved post-event tile is required before assigning a damage class.
vlm.before_source: Vantor Open Data pre-event reference scene B160001100FD1910, 2026-03-20T14:46:55Z, LG06, 1% cloud cover, not official EMS before imagery

vlm.change_evidence: The before image (Vantor) is sharp and shows a complex of structures with red/terracotta tile roofs, white-roofed buildings, trees, and streets within the yellow footprint. The after image is severely pixelated and blurry, with much lower effective resolution. Vague shapes consistent with buildings can be perceived within the outline, but no clear damage indicators (rubble fields, exposed floors, collapse, debris shadows) can be resolved. The dramatic resolution/quality drop prevents reliable change detection.
vlm.before_observation: Clear aerial scene centered on a built-up urban block. Within the yellow outline: several building footprints with prominent red/terracotta roofs, at least one lighter (white/grey) roof, narrow streets/gaps between structures, and patches of vegetation/trees. Reticle sits over a roofed structure. Surrounding area shows a large red-roofed complex to the south and lighter buildings to the west.
vlm.after_observation: Heavily pixelated, low-resolution scene of the same approximate area. The yellow outline is still visible and roughly aligns with the before footprint. Faint rectangular/linear shapes suggest some built structures may still be present, but the red roofs and fine architectural detail from the before image are not resolvable. No clearly visible rubble, debris fields, or collapse patterns can be confirmed or ruled out due to pixelation.
vlm.image_alignment: Coarse alignment appears consistent — the yellow footprint overlays the same general area and the reticle is at the same nominal centroid. However, the after image's much lower spatial resolution makes any pixel-level or feature-level comparison unreliable.
vlm.image_quality: Before: high-resolution, sharp, 1% cloud cover, nadir-like. After: very low effective resolution, heavily blocky/pixelated, no cloud cover, but quality is insufficient for damage assessment. The resolution mismatch is the dominant issue.
vlm.uncertainty_reason: The after chip's resolution is dramatically lower than the before chip (severe pixelation), so that even though a footprint exists in roughly the same location, the VLM cannot resolve roofs, rubble, or collapse to confirm or deny damage. A human analyst with the native-resolution EMS post-event product is required before any triage decision. Do not overrule the EMS 'Possibly damaged / 35%' label without that check.
